portfolio: Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport & Arts
executive_summary: The NMA is a corporate Commonwealth collecting institution operating on a constrained budget supplemented by the 2023-24 $78.3m National Collecting Institutions funding package, with commercial and philanthropic revenue an explicit growth ambition. The plan's strongest, most time-bound signals are operational: a digital uplift that will procure and implement a Trusted Digital Repository and a new Content Management System to replace ageing infrastructure, delivery of a Strategic Asset Management Plan against an end-of-life HVAC and critical building systems, and financial-sustainability pressure under cost-of-living and global economic uncertainty. Multi-year capital ambitions (Acton site precinct planning, Landmarks gallery refresh, First Australians gallery redevelopment for which it will 'seek additional funding') create a pipeline of program and assurance work. Note a headwind: under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework the Museum is actively reducing outsourcing of core work in Engineering/Technical and Service Delivery job families, so offerings must target genuinely specialist, project-shaped need and be right-sized to a small institution.

strategic_priorities.title: Digital and technology transformation
strategic_priorities.description: Replace ageing digital infrastructure and uplift digital capability by procuring and implementing a Trusted Digital Repository (TDR) and a new Content Management System (CMS), upgrading legacy systems, defining a cloud/system-ownership approach and maintaining cybersecurity maturity.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Implement digital uplift projects: Procure and implement Trusted Digital Repository (TDR); Procure and implement new Content Management System (CMS).

strategic_priorities.title: Asset management and critical infrastructure
strategic_priorities.description: Deliver a Strategic Asset Management Plan (SAMP) to assess future asset and funding needs, and progress urgent replacement of end-of-life plant including the HVAC system, building management and fire-safety systems.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Secure the Museum's building and operations through strategic asset management; Deliver Strategic Asset Management Plan (SAMP).

strategic_priorities.title: Funding and own-source revenue growth
strategic_priorities.description: Grow commercial operations (retail, venue hire, tourism, e-commerce, ticketing) and philanthropy to build a sustainable revenue base, while managing cost pressure and uncertain visitation.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Maintain sustained growth and profitability in current commercial operations... with renewed focus and investment in growing e-Commerce and exploring new commercial opportunities.

strategic_priorities.title: Acton site and gallery redevelopment
strategic_priorities.description: Undertake strategic planning for the Acton Peninsula precinct, refresh the Landmarks gallery, and progress the multi-year First Australians gallery redevelopment (subject to additional funding).
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Develop an ambitious plan for the Acton Peninsula that places the Museum at the centre of a vibrant cultural and creative precinct.

strategic_priorities.title: First Nations engagement and capability
strategic_priorities.description: Stand up the new First Nations division, develop a Cultural Plan, renew the Reconciliation Action Plan, deliver cultural-capability programs and grow First Nations workforce representation.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: A new First Nations division has been created, with strategic responsibility for engaging broadly and deeply with First Nations people.

strategic_priorities.title: Touring program reset
strategic_priorities.description: Implement a new modular touring model commencing 2025, informed by a domestic-venue survey, to expand regional and metropolitan access to the collection.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The new model, which will commence delivery in 2025, adopts a modular approach to touring that will increase access to our programs for regional and metropolitan audiences.

reform_and_change_drivers: National Cultural Policy (Revive) and the Minister for the Arts' Statement of Expectations shaping strategic priorities
2023-24 Budget funding package of $78.3m over four years for National Collecting Institutions, tied to effective resource management and transparent reporting
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework driving insourcing of core work and FTE/expenditure reduction targets in Engineering/Technical and Service Delivery job families
APS Net Zero 2030 emissions target and the Museum's Environmental Sustainability Action Plan (HVAC replacement, solar, gas removal, EV fleet)
Organisational realignment creating a new First Nations division
Growth in digital engagement (2.7m in 2018-19 to 7.4m in 2024-25) against ageing digital infrastructure
Cost-of-living and global economic uncertainty pressuring visitation and own-source revenue
challenges_and_risks: Funding and financial sustainability: insufficient funds to pursue all strategic objectives; reliance on own-source and philanthropic revenue
End-of-life critical infrastructure (HVAC, building management, fire safety) requiring major works beyond existing capital funding
Ageing ICT/digital infrastructure and legacy systems needing modernisation and a defined cloud approach
IT and cybersecurity risk - maintaining Essential Eight maturity against unauthorised access and disruption
Deterioration/loss of National Historical Collection objects and storage capacity constraints
Critical skills and capability gaps in a workforce expected to deliver expanding strategic priorities while reducing outsourcing
Visitation and revenue volatility dependent on blockbuster international exhibitions

news_and_controversies.summary: The Canberra Times reported (c.2020-21) that the NMA would cut jobs following a COVID-19-driven revenue drop, and an earlier (2019) inquiry was launched into Canberra's national museums and galleries after funding and staff cuts.
news_and_controversies.implication: Confirms a recurring own-source revenue and operating-funding fragility narrative across the national collecting institutions - relevant to financial-sustainability and commercial-revenue advisory - but both items predate the current 3-year window and should be re-verified against current finances.

opportunity_areas.area: Digital uplift: TDR and CMS implementation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Replace ageing digital infrastructure by procuring and implementing a Trusted Digital Repository and a new Content Management System, plus legacy-system and cloud platform modernisation.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and solution design followed by implementation/integration of the TDR and CMS - including business and process analysis, vendor/platform selection support and service transition - scaled to a small institution. The firm's ability to build and implement (not just advise) is the differentiator.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Named, time-bound 2025-26 deliverables ('Procure and implement TDR... Procure and implement new CMS') backed by the ICT Strategic Roadmap and explicit acknowledgement of ageing infrastructure and rising digital demand (7.4m engagements).

opportunity_areas.area: Procurement support for digital and infrastructure buys
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Run sound approaches to market for the TDR, CMS and major plant replacement (HVAC) within tight funding and transparent-reporting expectations.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Procurement strategy, evaluation support and non-legal probity advisory/probity audit for the digital procurements and the HVAC/critical-infrastructure works, ensuring defensible process for funding tied to 'effective management of all our resources'.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly states the Museum will 'procure and implement' TDR and CMS and is planning major plant replacement; funding is conditional on transparent, well-managed resourcing.

opportunity_areas.area: Strategic Asset Management Plan and capital pipeline
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver a SAMP that provides a comprehensive program of future asset and funding needs, and build the funding case for major works (HVAC, First Australians gallery) that exceed current capital funding.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability and capital-funding review supporting the SAMP - lifecycle costing of end-of-life assets, prioritised investment program and a funding submission/business case to government for unfunded major works.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to delivering the SAMP in 2025-26 and states major works 'cannot currently be managed within existing capital funding' and that it 'continues to work with government to identify and address funding pressures'.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Grow commercial and philanthropic revenue and manage cost pressure to sustain operations amid economic uncertainty.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review with a commercial-revenue and e-commerce growth model, including activity-based costing of commercial operations and scenario analysis of visitation-driven revenue risk.
opportunity_areas.rationale: 'Funding and financial sustainability' is a named enterprise risk; the Growth priority targets commercial profitability and a $575,000 philanthropy target, with own-source revenue strategies flagged as a mitigation.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Maintain cybersecurity maturity and Essential Eight controls against unauthorised access and disruption as systems are modernised.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment against the Essential Eight maturity model and cyber advisory/assurance of the new TDR and CMS as they are stood up, sized to a small institution.
opportunity_areas.rationale: IT and cybersecurity is the first-listed enterprise risk, with explicit reliance on ACSC guidelines and Essential Eight; new platforms create fresh assurance need.

opportunity_areas.area: Gallery and Acton precinct program delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver the Landmarks gallery refresh and progress multi-year First Australians gallery redevelopment and Acton site strategic planning.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program/PMO support and delivery assurance for the gallery redevelopment and Acton precinct planning, including a benefits-management framework to underpin future funding submissions.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the Landmarks refresh as a 2025-26 deliverable and flags the First Australians gallery as 'a major multi-year project for which we will seek additional funding', alongside Acton strategic planning.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Make more collection objects available online and improve information governance and management of digital collection assets.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Information strategy and data-management support aligned to the TDR rollout - improving digitisation, metadata and online accessibility of the National Historical Collection.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The Connection priority targets increasing collections available online via the EMu system, and the Museum has an information governance framework under regular review; the TDR underpins this.

recommended_actions.action: Request a meeting with the head of ICT/Digital (and the COO) to present a TDR/CMS discovery-to-implementation approach, leading with the firm's build-and-implement capability rather than advice-only.
recommended_actions.rationale: These are explicit, funded 2025-26 procure-and-implement deliverables and the clearest near-term opportunity in the plan.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a tailored capability statement on capital/asset-funding business cases and lifecycle costing for the CFO/COO ahead of the SAMP delivery and the unfunded HVAC and First Australians gallery works.
recommended_actions.rationale: The SAMP is a 2025-26 deliverable and the plan openly states major works exceed current capital funding - a credible, time-aligned financial advisory entry point.

recommended_actions.action: Offer a short non-legal probity and procurement-strategy proposition for the digital procurements, positioned around the funding's transparent-management conditions.
recommended_actions.rationale: Multiple named procurements are imminent and funding is conditional on demonstrable good resource management; probity is a low-sensitivity, easy-to-win first engagement.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a brief on Essential Eight assessment and assurance of new platforms, timed to the TDR/CMS build.
recommended_actions.rationale: Cyber is the top enterprise risk and new systems trigger assurance need; a small, defined scope suits the institution's budget.

recommended_actions.action: Watch for the Acton precinct strategic plan and any First Australians gallery funding announcement as triggers for program-delivery and assurance work.
recommended_actions.rationale: These are multi-year, currently unfunded ambitions; engagement value materialises once funding is secured.

engagement_targets: Chief Operating Officer / CFO (financial sustainability, SAMP, capital funding)
Head of ICT / Digital function (ICT Strategic Roadmap, TDR, CMS, cloud, cyber)
Asset management / facilities function (HVAC and critical infrastructure)
Deputy Director First Nations (Cultural Plan, RAP, cultural capability - relationship building, culturally led)
Audit, Finance and Risk Committee (assurance and enterprise risk oversight)
Director / Council (major program and funding priorities)
talking_points: Your plan commits to procuring and implementing both a Trusted Digital Repository and a new CMS in 2025-26 - we can take this from discovery and design through to build, integration and transition, not just advise.

With the SAMP due this year and major works like the HVAC replacement sitting outside current capital funding, we can help lifecycle-cost the program and build a defensible funding submission to government.

Your 2023-24 funding package is tied to effective resource management and transparent reporting - independent procurement and non-legal probity support makes that demonstrable for the digital and infrastructure buys.
Cyber is your top enterprise risk and you're standing up new platforms - a right-sized Essential Eight assessment and assurance of the new systems protects the investment.

We understand you're insourcing core Engineering/Technical and Service Delivery work under the Strategic Commissioning Framework - our offerings are scoped to genuinely specialist, project-shaped needs that complement, not displace, your internal capability.
caveats: This is a small corporate collecting institution with a modest budget, so all offerings must be right-sized - short advisory and design pieces, or scaled digital implementation, not large embedded programs. A material headwind is the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework: the Museum is explicitly reducing outsourcing and setting FTE/expenditure reduction targets, so any pitch must target specialist, project-shaped demand. External research was incomplete: the search tool capped before ANAO and Parliament could be searched, so those sections are empty and should be re-run; the only news items found predate the 3-year window and are included only as contextual evidence of recurring funding fragility. Several opportunities (data/information, gallery program delivery) depend on funding the Museum has yet to secure and are marked lower confidence accordingly.
